title: I Saw Thy Form In Youthful Prime.
content:
I saw thy form in youthful prime,
        Nor thought that pale decay
    Would steal before the steps of Time,
        And waste its bloom away, Mary!

    Yet still thy features wore that light,
        Which fleets not with the breath;
    And life ne'er looked more truly bright
        Than in thy smile of death, Mary!

    As streams that run o'er golden mines,
        Yet humbly, calmly glide,
    Nor seem to know the wealth that shines
        Within their gentle tide, Mary!
    So veiled beneath the simplest guise,
        Thy radiant genius shone,
    And that, which charmed all other eyes,
        Seemed worthless in thy own, Mary!

    If souls could always dwell above,
        Thou ne'er hadst left that sphere;
    Or could we keep the souls we love,
        We ne'er had lost thee here, Mary!
    Though many a gifted mind we meet,
        Though fairest forms we see,
    To live with them is far less sweet,
        Than to remember thee, Mary!
meaning:
This evocative piece by Thomas Moore, titled "I Saw Thy Form In Youthful Prime.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
